visa_policy.notes: Visa policy sourced from the official Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office PDF (dated 22 June 2022) and the live Visa Requirements page (passport.govmu.org, verified June 2026). The main exemptions page lists: citizens of Mauritius; residents of Mauritius; spouse/child of citizens/residents; vessel crew members on duty or transiting; holders of UN/COMESA/SADC/other international organization laissez-passer; INTERPOL passport holders on official mission; holders of laissez-passer from African Reinsurance Corporation and African Development Bank Group travelling on official mission; EU citizen passport holders; transiting passengers remaining only during vessel stay. Countries requiring visa prior to travel (not on arrival): Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Iraq, Laos, Libya, Mali, North Korea, Pakistan, Palestinian State, Saharawi Republic, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Yemen. Guyana: specifically noted on the Visa Requirements page as requiring visa prior to travel. Tourist visa allows up to 6 months per calendar year. Visa is issued free of charge. An Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) system was announced by the Prime Minister in December 2025 but has not yet launched; scope and applicable nationalities are undefined.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for stays up to 90 days. Exemption does NOT apply to passport holders from: Afghanistan, Iraq, Kosovo, Libya, Palestine State, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Yemen.

Bilateral Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the Republic of Cote d'Ivoire on reciprocal exemption of entry visas for holders of diplomatic and official passports. Nationals of both countries holding valid diplomatic or service passports are exempted from visa entry requirements for stays of less than 90 days. Applicable to persons assigned to a Diplomatic Mission, Consular Post, Permanent Mission and Organisation in the other State or attending meetings and conferences. Cabinet approved signing on 28 March 2025; formal entry into force date not yet confirmed on official Mauritius immigration page.

cbi.program_name: Naturalization for Investors (Section 9(3) Mauritius Citizenship Act)

options.notes: Under section 9(3) of the Mauritius Citizenship Act, an investor may be naturalized as a citizen if: (i) he has invested a sum of not less than USD 500,000 in Mauritius; and (ii) he has resided in Mauritius for a continuous period of not less than 2 years preceding the date of application. This is naturalization-by-investment (investor route), not a standalone CBI program with a direct donation option. Application submitted to the Secretary for Home Affairs; declaration made before a Supreme Court Judge or Magistrate; applicants must place advertisements in two daily newspapers.

cbi.notes: Mauritius does not have a dedicated citizenship-by-investment program (no donation-only CBI). The only investment pathway to citizenship is via naturalization under Section 9(3) of the Mauritius Citizenship Act, which requires both a minimum investment of USD 500,000 AND 2 years of continuous residency. Dual citizenship policy not explicitly stated on the official DHA citizenship page. Contact: pmo.citizenship@govmu.org.

programs.name: Occupation Permit – Investor Category (Option 1)
programs.notes: Minimum investment of USD 50,000. Occupation Permit valid for 10 years, renewable. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after at least 5 years as Investor OP holder with cumulative turnover of MUR 20 million over 5 years (MUR 1.5 million in year 1). Year 6+ renewal requires MUR 5 million annual turnover. Application fee USD 1,000 upon approval; USD 50 non-refundable application fee. Updated August 2025.

programs.name: Occupation Permit – Investor Category (Option 2)
programs.notes: Minimum investment of USD 100,000. Occupation Permit valid for 10 years, renewable. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after at least 5 years with cumulative turnover of MUR 15 million over 5 years (MUR 1 million in year 1). Year 6+ renewal requires MUR 5 million annual turnover. Higher investment threshold with lower turnover target compared to Option 1. Updated August 2025.

programs.name: Permanent Residence Permit (PRP) – Real Estate Investment
programs.notes: An investor who invests at least USD 375,000 in a qualifying business activity is eligible to apply for the 20-year Permanent Residence Permit. Foreign nationals may also acquire residential property under IRS, RES, PDS or Smart City Scheme (SCS) to obtain residency. 20-year PRP is renewable. Exact minimum purchase prices for IRS/RES/PDS/SCS schemes require verification on EDB/official property scheme pages.

programs.name: Retired Non-Citizen Residence Permit
programs.notes: Available to foreign nationals above 50 years of age. Occupation Permit (Retired) issued for up to 5 years (changed from 10 years effective August 2025). Requires transfer of at least USD 2,000 per month (USD 24,000 per year) to a Mauritian bank account and minimum 180 days per year residency in Mauritius. Eligible for 20-year Permanent Residence Permit after holding the Residence Permit for at least 5 years with total transfer of at least USD 200,000 over the 5-year period. Application fee USD 50 (non-refundable). CORRECTION: Prior record stated 10-year permit duration and USD 54,000 over 3 years; updated to reflect August 2025 rule changes.

programs.name: Occupation Permit – Professional Category
programs.notes: Allows foreign professionals employed by a company in Mauritius to work and reside. Minimum salary: MUR 30,000/month. Valid for up to 3 years or duration of employment contract. Eligible for 20-year PRP after at least 5 years with a basic monthly salary of at least MUR 400,000 for 5 consecutive years. Expert Pass tier available at MUR 250,000/month. CORRECTION: Prior record stated MUR 150,000/month for PRP and 3-year path to PR; updated per August 2025 rule changes.

programs.name: Expert Pass
programs.notes: New category introduced effective August 2025 for highly skilled professionals earning at least MUR 250,000/month. Administered through NELS (National Electronic Licensing System). Full eligibility criteria and PRP pathway not yet confirmed on official Passport Immigration Office page.

programs.name: Occupation Permit – Self-Employed Category
programs.notes: Effective August 2025: minimum investment of USD 50,000 in services sector only. Must provide letters of intent from at least three clients (two local). Annual income targets: MUR 750,000 in year 1, scaling to MUR 6 million cumulative over 5 years. Occupation Permit issued for up to 10 years. Eligible for 20-year PRP after at least 5 years with annual business income of at least MUR 3 million for 5 consecutive years. Application online via NELS. CORRECTION: Prior record did not specify services-sector restriction or updated turnover targets.

programs.name: Young Professional Occupation Permit (YPOP)
programs.notes: For foreign students who have completed at least an undergraduate degree at a Mauritius institution recognized by the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC). Application must be submitted not later than 6 months after publication of results. Business activity must fall within: Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Fintech, Robotics, Financial Services, Information Technology. Effective August 2025: valid for maximum 2 years (reduced from 3 years), minimum salary MUR 25,000/month. After 2 years, must transition to standard Occupation Permit. Established under Section 13 of the Economic Development Board Act 2017 and Section 9A of the Immigration Act 1970. CORRECTION: Prior record stated 3 years; updated per August 2025 rule changes.

programs.name: Family Occupation Permit
programs.notes: Established under section 14 of the Immigration Act 2022 and section 13(1)(b) of the Economic Development Board Act 2017. Allows an applicant, spouse, dependent child, parent, other dependent, or person working exclusively for the family unit to become a resident for 10 years.

programs.name: Premium Visa (Premium Travel Visa)
programs.eligibility: Nationals from 180+ countries including the US, UK, Canada, Australia, France, Germany, Japan, Singapore, UAE and most European nations. Applicant must not enter the Mauritius labour market; main place of business and source of income must be outside Mauritius; must have proof of long stay plans and sufficient travel and health insurance.
programs.processing_time: Online application; visa issued by email after review
programs.notes: Valid for a period exceeding 6 months up to 1 year, renewable. For stays over 180 days in a calendar year. Free of charge (no processing fee). Citizens from countries not on the eligible list may apply online during a tourist visa stay. Administered by EDB Mauritius. No specific minimum income requirement stated. CORRECTION: Prior record stated 'approximately 130 countries'; updated to 180+ per official EDB page verified June 2026.

agreements: COMESA (Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa) – Mauritius is a member; holders of COMESA laissez-passer are exempt from Mauritius visa requirements
SADC (Southern African Development Community) – Mauritius is a member; holders of SADC laissez-passer are exempt from Mauritius visa requirements

validation_notes: CORRECTED (June 2026): Retired Non-Citizen Residence Permit duration changed from 10 years to 5 years, per August 2025 immigration rule changes. Minimum transfer requirement updated from USD 54,000 over 3 years to USD 24,000/year (USD 2,000/month), with 180 days/year residency required. PRP eligibility path extended from 3 years to 5 years, with total transfer of USD 200,000 over 5 years.

CORRECTED (June 2026): Occupation Permit – Professional Category: minimum salary threshold updated to MUR 30,000/month; PRP eligibility path extended from 3 to 5 years; PRP salary threshold updated from MUR 150,000/month to MUR 400,000/month, per August 2025 changes.

CORRECTED (June 2026): Occupation Permit – Investor Category: now two options (USD 50,000 and USD 100,000) with differentiated turnover targets (MUR 20M vs MUR 15M cumulative over 5 years); PRP eligibility now 5 years (not unspecified).
CORRECTED (June 2026): Self-Employed OP: now restricted to services sector, USD 50,000 minimum investment, updated turnover targets (MUR 750K year 1, MUR 6M cumulative over 5 years).
CORRECTED (June 2026): Young Professional OP: validity reduced from 3 years to 2 years, minimum salary MUR 25,000/month per August 2025 changes.
ADDED (June 2026): Expert Pass — new high-skill tier at MUR 250,000/month introduced August 2025.
CORRECTED (June 2026): Premium Visa eligible countries updated from 'approximately 130' to '180+' per official EDB residency.mu page.
ADDED (June 2026): ETA announcement noted — Prime Minister announced ETA system in December 2025; not yet launched as of June 2026.

CONFIRMED (June 2026): Visa_free and visa_on_arrival country lists and structure match the 2022 official PDF and live official page as of June 2026. No new nationality-level changes to visa_free/VoA found on official sources.
CONFIRMED (June 2026): CBI — USD 500,000 + 2 years residency requirement confirmed against live dha.govmu.org page.
CONFIRMED (June 2026): Conditional_access diplomatic/official passport exemption (90 days, exclusion list) confirmed against live passport.govmu.org page.
gaps: The visa requirements PDF from passport.govmu.org is dated 22 June 2022; a more recent version may exist on the official site. The max_stay_days for 'No visa required' entries are not stated explicitly in the official PDF. Tourist visa duration is up to 6 months per calendar year per other official pages, but specific per-nationality stay limits for visa-free entries are not enumerated in the official country-wise list.
Guyana is specifically noted on the Visa Requirements page as requiring a visa prior to travel (not listed in the country-wise PDF table), so it is excluded from visa_free and visa_on_arrival.

An ETA system was announced by Prime Minister Ramgoolam in December 2025 (visasnews.com). Scope, applicable nationalities, launch date, and fee structure remain undefined on official Mauritius government pages as of June 2026.
No standalone donation-based CBI program found on official sources; only the naturalization-by-investment pathway under Section 9(3) of the Citizenship Act.
Dual citizenship policy for investor naturalization applicants not explicitly stated on the official DHA citizenship page.
Processing time for investor naturalization (CBI pathway) not stated on official page.
Exact minimum purchase prices for IRS/RES/PDS/SCS real estate schemes not stated on the official Passport Immigration Office page; EDB/official property scheme pages required for exact figures.

No official Mauritius government source found for credential-based conditional entry rules (i.e., no rule granting visa-free/VoA/e-visa entry based on holding a valid US, UK, Schengen, Canada, Australia, GCC, or other foreign visa or residence permit).
No OCI or equivalent special-status cardholder entry privilege found on official Mauritius immigration sources.

Wikipedia reports that Mainland China diplomatic/official/service passport holders receive a 60-day (not 90-day) exemption; this could not be verified on an official Mauritius government page and is therefore not recorded in conditional_access.

Cote d'Ivoire bilateral diplomatic/official passport agreement (conditional_access entry 2): Cabinet approved signing on 28 March 2025 (pmo.govmu.org official) but formal entry-into-force date not confirmed on the Mauritius Passport and Immigration Office website.

A Ukraine–Mauritius general visa waiver agreement was reportedly signed on 8 August 2024 (per Interfax Ukraine). Ukraine is already listed as visa-free for ordinary passports. Formal confirmation on official Mauritius government page not found.
Expert Pass (MUR 250,000/month) full eligibility criteria and PRP pathway not yet published on official Passport Immigration Office page; sourced from third-party immigration advisory (ibn.co.za).

August 2025 RBI changes (retirement permit, professional OP, YPOP, investor OP options, self-employed) sourced from immigration advisory (ibn.co.za/visaverge.com); exact implementing regulations on official govmu.org or EDB Mauritius pages not directly fetched — should be verified against official NELS/EDB portal when available.

outbound_official.notes: Source is the official Mauritius Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade PDF titled 'VISA REQUIREMENTS FOR MAURITIAN NATIONALS TRAVELLING ABROAD', updated 17 November 2025, published at foreign.govmu.org. The document covers 203 destinations. Visa-free entries reflect countries listed as 'Visa not required' or equivalent. Visa-on-arrival entries reflect countries listed as 'Visa on arrival'. E-visa entries reflect countries requiring an electronic visa obtained prior to or upon departure via an online portal (Cambodia e-visa, Sri Lanka ETA, Togo e-visa, Turkey e-visa). Serbia and UK require an ETA. UK also allows visa-free entry up to 6 months but requires an ETA for short-term visits if no valid visa is held. Rodrigues Island is an autonomous island within Mauritius; it is listed in the document as 'Visa not required'. Countries requiring visa prior to departure (not included in the above categories) include: Afghanistan, Algeria, Argentina, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, Belarus, Bhutan, Bolivia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Brazil, Brunei Darussalam, Burkina Faso, Canada, Cape Verde, Central African Republic, China (Taiwan), Colombia, Cuba, Democratic Republic of Congo, Dominican Republic, Egypt, El Salvador, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Guatemala, Guinea, Guinea Bissau, Guyana, Haiti, Honduras, India, Iraq, Ireland, Kazakhstan, Korea (North), Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Lebanon, Liberia, Libya, Macedonia, Mali, Marshall Islands, Mauritania, Mexico, Micronesia, Moldova, Monaco, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, Myanmar, New Zealand, Niger, Oman, Pakistan, Papua New Guinea, Paraguay, Peru, Sao Tome and Principe, Saudi Arabia, Sierra Leone, Solomon Islands, Sudan, South Sudan, Suriname, Syria, Tajikistan, Tanzania (visa-free but note: document says 'Visa not required' with exemption from visa fees for SADC nationals), Tchad, Timor Leste, Tonga, Turkmenistan, Ukraine, Uruguay, USA, Uzbekistan, Vanuatu, Vatican, Venezuela, Vietnam, Yemen.
